name: Stuffed Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup soft, fine whole-wheat bread crumbs
1 box frozen chopped spinach (10 ounce), thawed and thoroughly drained (save liquid for soup stock)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees.
2. In a nonstick or Pam-sprayed skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
3. Add the onion and sauté for 6 minutes, or until softened, translucent, limp, and just starting to brown around the edges.
4. Add garlic, lower the heat slightly, and sauté for an additional 2 minutes, stirring.
5. Add bread crumbs, herbs, and spinach. Stir well and remove from heat.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and add egg and Parmesan cheese.
7. When the mixture is cool enough to handle, stuff it into the prepared tomato shells. Stuff firmly and round the filling over the tops of tomatoes.
8. Place tomatoes on a Pam-sprayed baking sheet.
9. Bake for 20 minutes, or until the tops are slightly browned and crunchy. Serve hot or at room temperature.
